mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and consumption capacity, which supports corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This is an always-on trend signal with no activation threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage_value=152383.0 as of 2019-12-01 showing continued expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month trailing change and 20d forward SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or positive PAYEMS months precede negative forward returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals an inflection into labor market deterioration that historically precedes broader economic slowdown, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5% vintage 2019-12-01, historically low and stable
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tightening labor market
failure_condition: Following triggered UNRATE increases, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of historical trigger instances, or the effect reverses sign in post-2010 subsample.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between the level of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation determines the real policy rate; when nominal FEDFUNDS remains below realized CPI inflation (negative real rate), financial conditions are effectively accommodative, and equities respond positively over the next month even if nominal rates are stable. Neither series alone captures this; the joint level matters.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=1.55% vs CPIAUCSL YoY growth implying near-zero real rate as of late 2019
regime_hint: low nominal rate environment with moderate inflation
failure_condition: Rank IC of the real-rate proxy (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL YoY) with 20d forward returns is indistinguishable from the FEDFUNDS-only single-factor model (delta IC < 0.01) across the full sample, invalidating the interaction claim.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) are a leading indicator of consumer demand and construction-sector activity; sustained increases in HOUST during a housing-expansion regime forecast broader economic acceleration and positive equity returns over the following quarter, but this relationship weakens or reverses when housing is late-cycle overbuilt.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage=1365.0 thousand units Nov 2019, elevated versus post-crisis lows
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with HOUST 6-month trend during periods identified as housing expansion (pre-2007 and 2012-2019 subsamples), or correlation flips negative in more than one of these subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) month-over-month acceleration reflects improving manufacturing throughput and supply-side capacity utilization, which feeds directly into near-term corporate revenue signals and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week. This is an always-on effect independent of regime.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value=109.677 Nov 2019 prior to large subsequent downward revision
failure_condition: 5-day forward return Rank IC with INDPRO monthly change is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, indicating no short-horizon predictive value.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above the trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation rate (real policy rate turns positive) after a period of negative real rates, it signals a genuine tightening of financial conditions that raises the equity discount rate and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL YoY crosses from negative to positive
evidence_refs: fact:real rate near zero as of Dec 2019, close to potential crossing point
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle transitioning to restrictive real rates
failure_condition: In historical instances of the real-rate crossing above zero, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, contradicting the tightening-drag mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicates simultaneous strength in consumer spending capacity and income generation, a combination that more reliably signals sustained consumption-driven GDP growth than either series alone, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth > 1%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage_value=527994.0 Nov 2019 showing continued consumer strength
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes produce 20d forward returns not significantly different from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or single-series RSAFS-only model achieves equal or higher Rank IC, invalidating the interaction claim.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, deceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) below its trailing 4-quarter average signals exhaustion of the expansion phase and forecasts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as forward earnings growth expectations are downgraded; this relationship is weak or absent during early-cycle recoveries.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage_value=19121.112 (2019-07-01) later heavily revised to 20843.322, indicating high revision risk in late-cycle GDP readings
failure_condition: GDPC1 deceleration episodes identified in real time (using vintage, not revised, data) fail to precede negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of late-cycle instances, or the effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero after accounting for GDP revision noise.
